question.text: In which derivative segment does SCRA Section 4 (exchange recognition) apply, and which regulator grants such recognition?

answer.text: Section 4 applies to all exchange-traded derivative segments (equity, currency, IR), and SEBI grants recognition to the exchanges
Section 4 applies only to equity derivative exchanges; currency and IR exchanges are recognized by RBI
Section 4 applies only to currency derivative exchanges; equity exchanges are recognized under the Companies Act
Section 4 has been repealed and exchange recognition is now governed entirely by SEBI Act provisions

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SCRA Section 4 governs the recognition of stock exchanges. ALL derivative exchanges — whether trading equity, currency, or interest rate derivatives — must be recognized under this section. SEBI is the authority that grants this recognition, even for segments where RBI co-regulates the underlying. **The Trap:** Even though RBI co-regulates currency and IR derivatives, exchange recognition is SEBI's domain under SCRA Section 4. RBI does not grant exchange recognition. **Cross-Exam Context:** SCRA is the common legal backbone across all exchange-traded segments. Section 4 (recognition) and Section 18A (derivative legality) apply universally. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The exchange is always SEBI's domain. RBI's role is limited to the underlying market, participant eligibility, and position limits — not exchange infrastructure.
